query: How much is the tuition fee at the University of Vienna?

queries: How much is the tuition fee at the University of Vienna?

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text:
There is a tuition fee and a Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and on whether you are enrolled in a degree programme or non-degree programme. The amount of the Students' Union fee is directly determined by the Austrian Students' Union. All students in Austria pay the Students' Union fee each semester to re-enrol in their degree programme and continue their studies.

To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Citizens of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from paying the tuition fee for a specified time period. This means: You only have to pay the Students' Union fee during the stipulated degree programme duration plus two tolerance semesters

Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can check how many semesters of the tuition-free period you have left in u:space > Studies > Study overview.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

As soon as the fee is marked as "received" it is registered as paid. Only then you can download your student documents. Make sure to choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents. If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another Austrian educational institution, you have to [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at the University of Vienna yourself within the payment period. Only then you can download your student documents. - What happens if I pay too late or too little? If you do not pay the full fee until the end of the payment period your degree programme will be automatically closed. You have realised that you have paid too little in due time (= within the payment period)? Transfer the remaining difference as soon as possible with the correct payment data. The required data (IBAN and BIC of the University of Vienna and your unique payment reference) is available in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You are not able to or do not want to pay the remaining difference? Please contact us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/706). We can remit the part of the fee you have already

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: If you study at the University of Vienna (and not any other Austrian educational institution) you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee here each semester. You do not have to complete any other steps. As soon as the prescribed fee has been received, you are re-enrolled in your studies. Then you can download your student documents in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) and validate your [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) (or order and then validate it if it is your first semester). / - Which payment options are available at the University of Vienna? You have four options how you can pay your tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna: 1. Eps-payment via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via eps-payment. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. The eps-payment is only possible with Austrian bank account. 2. Credit card (only Visa and Mastercard) via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via credit card. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. In the info box in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee you can find all data you need

It may take up to seven days until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Please note: Your unique payment reference changes each semester. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the University of Vienna. You therefore are also able to take examinations from courses at the University of Vienna that took place during your semester abroad (e.g. at a later examination date). If you have to pay the tuition fee, you can apply for [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) during your semester abroad. This is only possible during the semester in which your stay abroad takes place, not in retrospect. - I have dual citizenship. Which tuition fee do I have to pay? If your citizenship has changed, please message us for a [change of personal data](/en/change-of-personal-data/). - I study at two public universities in Austria. Where do I have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee? You can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text:
Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester. Thereby you automatically re-enrol in your studies. If you have already paid at another Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your degree programme yourself.

- You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period.
- [How much you have to pay](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and type of studies.
- Pay the exact fee (neither too much nor too little). You can find the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
- Wait until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Then you can download your student documents in u:space and validate your student ID, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).
- If you pay at another Austrian university of university of applied sciences, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself via u:space each semester.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

retrieved.title: Tuition fee/Students' Union fee
retrieved.text:
As long as you study, you must pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. In the first semester, you complete the admission procedure by paying the fee. In later semesters you thereby re-register for your degree programme.

Choose the applicable question below. You will be forwarded to a new page where you can find the detailed information you need.

[## How much do I have to pay?](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/)

[## How can I pay?](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/)

[## How do I apply for remission or reimbursement?](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/)

[## Am I eligible for equal status with EU/EEA citizens?](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/)

If you are currently attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU), you

- first pay the course fee at the VWU and afterwards
- the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

[Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- [Decree regarding the tuition fee 2004](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010724)
- [§ 23 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/studienrecht/)
- [Students' Union Act 2014](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)
- [Decree on Groups of Persons 2018](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010594)

- [Information on the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/)
- [Application and admission periods](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Administrative fee for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/administration-fee/)

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text:
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee each semester within the payment period. The amount of the tuition fee does not depend on your citizenship. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Please note: a non-degree programme does not lead to a graduation with an academic degree.

You are in Austria with a residence permit? Please contact the MA 35 if examinations from a non-degree programme can be recognised for your residence permit. Supplementary examinations from the VWU are recognised.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How much is the tuition fee?
retrieved.text:
Students who reside in Austria as asylum seekers, recognised refugees (persons entitled to asylum, not displaced persons), or people granted subsidiary protection may take a discounted German language course in the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) if they fulfil all admission requirements. You will be admitted as a non-degree student while you attend the VWU. During this time, you are only required to pay the [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).

Otherwise, the tuition fee for degree programmes depends on your citizenship. Asylum seekers, convention refugees, and people granted subsidiary protection may file for [equal status with EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) and, if granted, are then exempt from paying the tuition fee for the standard duration of your study programme + two tolerance semesters for a bachelor/diploma or master programme. During this time, you are only required to pay the Students’ Union fee.

Before commencing your studies, please consider how starting a degree programme may impact your financial situation or benefits. Different regulations apply depending on your student or resident status.

- [Allowance ("Grundversorgung") for asylum seekers](https://www.fluechtlinge.wien/grundversorgung/) (in German)
- [Needs-based minimum benefit for recognised refugees and people granted subsidiary protection](https://www.wien.gv.at/gesundheit/leistungen/mindestsicherung/) (in German)
- [Study grant for recognised refugees by the Austrian Study Grant Authority](https://www.stipendium.at/stipendien/studienbeihilfe) (in German)
- [Austrian Database for Scholarships and Research Grants](https://grants.at/en/)

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities
retrieved.text:
As long as you attend the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) you have to pay the course fee at the VWU first. Only then you can pay the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

- [Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text: can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree programme at the University of Vienna. Please note: If a higher fee is prescribed at one university, it is best to pay there directly. If you pay at the university where only the Students' Union fee is prescribed, you have to separately pay the remaining difference at the other university. For example: You only pay the Students' Union fee at another university. At the University of Vienna you have already exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. A few days after paying at the other university you can verify in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee if the Students' Union fee has already arrived. Then you pay the prescribed tuition fee at the University of Vienna. You only have to pay the tuition fee, the Students' Union fee is forwarded by the other university. - I am studying a degree programme and a continuing education or training programme at the Postgraduate Center. How much do I have to pay? If you only have to pay the Students' Union fee, you pay it with the programme fee for the Postgraduate Center. You do not have to

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text: / - I am EU/EEA citizen. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? Check [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Study overview to see which degree programmes you are admitted to. As soon as you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna all degree programmes are continued. It does not matter if you actively study them or not. The tuition fee is prescribed if you exceed the stipulated degree programme duration plus tolerance semesters. If a degree programme that you (currently) do not want to continue is in your study overview, [you can close it](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/). The amount of the tuition fee will then be calculated anew. - I have equal status. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? If you have equal status with EU/EEA citizens you are exempt from the tuition fee as long as - your residence permit is valid and - you are within the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. [Detailed information about equal status with EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) - Will my Erasmus+ semester be counted for the tuition-free period for EU/EEA citizens? Semesters during which you are abroad with a mobility programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union fee has been paid to the University of Vienna. If you pay at an FH: - Pay the prescribed fee. - Wait for a week. - Check if the Students' Union fee has been marked as "received" in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. - If this is the case you can [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at Studies > Study overview. Do not forget: If the tuition fee is also prescribed for you at the University of Vienna you also have to pay the remaining difference. - Should I pay with some kind of express payment, so the money is transferred faster? No, this is not necessary. The transfer is not much faster that way. An express payment does not really affect the process. The fastest way is paying directly via eps-payment (only possible with Austrian bank account) or credit card in u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students'

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. - Only ever use your latest unique payment reference when paying. Otherwise, we are not able to correctly allocate your payment. Your unique payment reference has 12 digits and consists of numbers and letters. You can find all data you need for the payment in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Your name, student ID number and semester are not relevant. If you pay via eps-payment or credit card via u:space all required data is already filled in. Do not change it. - Do not save your unique payment reference. The data changes each semester. - You can only pay within the payment period of the respective semester. Thereby you re-enrol in your studies. We recommend: Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the beginning of the payment period. Thereby you have the opportunity to act in good time, in case of an incorrect payment. - If the prescribed fee is not received at the University of Vienna in due time, your studies will be closed.

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
retrieved.text:
If you study at the University of Vienna you have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester in due time. If certain grounds are at hand, you can submit an application for remission. If you have already paid, you can apply for a reimbursement of the tuition fee provided you fulfil the applicable reasons.

If one of the reasons mentioned below applies to you, you submit your application for remission of the tuition fee within the application period.

We recommend: Submit your application for remission no later than one month before the deadline. The processing of your application may take several weeks. We will inform you about the outcome of your application as well as any further steps that may be required via e-mail to your u:account e-mail address.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
retrieved.text: the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - Application for leave of absence granted After paying the tuition fee your [application for leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) was granted? A reimbursement is only possible if you have submitted your application for leave of absence before 30 November (for the winter semester) and 30 April (for the summer semester). - Application for remission granted Your application for remission of the tuition fee was granted after paying the tuition fee? The tuition fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too high You have paid a too high fee? The difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too little You have paid too little and are therefore no longer admitted at the University of Vienna? The fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - Reimbursement of the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another university
retrieved.text: If you study at multiple universities in Austria, you can decide where you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee yourself. You have already paid at another university? Then you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself each semester. Otherwise your studies will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. / - How do I re-enrol in my studies if I pay at another university? 1. Pay the prescribed fee at the other university and wait for a few days. 2. Log into u:space within the payment period of the University of Vienna. 3. Verify if the fee is already marked as "received" at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. There may be a remaining difference left to pay. This may be the case if you have, for example, paid at an Austrian university of applied science but the tuition fee is prescribed at the University of Vienna. 4. Look for the degree programme you want to continue studying at Studies > Study overview. Confirm that you want to continue studying this degree programme. To do so choose the button "continue studying". If you want to continue studying multiple degree programmes you have to repeat this step for each programme respectively. 5. Refresh the page in u:space. In the study overview you can now see that you are re-enroled in your degree

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
retrieved.text: the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is possible if a reasons for reimbursement applies and - if you have completed or closed your degree programme and are also not admitted at any other Austrian university or - if you have paid the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna and also at another Austrian university/university of applied sciences for the same semester. You have accidently paid the Students' Union fee twice at the University of Vienna? If you have accidentally paid the Students' Union fee twice at the University of Vienna it only transferred once to the Austrian Students' Union. In this case you have to apply for reimbursement not at the Students' Union but directly at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges that will be deducted from your account! → Solution: Pay the fee again. Make sure to enter the correct data in the correct field. 2. You have paid a too high/too little fee. If you have paid too much (double payment, wrong prescribed fee) we can reimburse the difference to the required fee. → Solution: Submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). If you have paid to little you can pay the remaining difference until the end of the payment period. Make sure to enter the correct payment data. If the fee is not paid fully or in due time your degree programme will be closed. → Solution: Pay the remaining difference before the end of the payment period. 3. You have entered the unique payment reference (called "Semesterkundendaten" before) of a wrong semester If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges

In u:space > Personal matters > My documents > Payment request/confirmation you can find all data you need for the payment. Follow the instructions in the template to fill out the payment form. It may take up to seven days until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Please note: Your unique payment reference changes each semester. Only ever use the latest data. - What do I have to keep in mind when paying at the University of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be

reranked.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
reranked.text: programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the University of Vienna. You therefore are also able to take examinations from courses at the University of Vienna that took place during your semester abroad (e.g. at a later examination date). If you have to pay the tuition fee, you can apply for [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) during your semester abroad. This is only possible during the semester in which your stay abroad takes place, not in retrospect. - I have dual citizenship. Which tuition fee do I have to pay? You can only enter one citizenship in u:space. You have one citizenship from an EU/EAA country or Switzerland and another from a non-EU/EEA country? Enter the citizenship that is more beneficial for you. If your citizenship has changed, please message us for a [change of personal data](/en/change-of-personal-data/). - I study at two public universities in Austria. Where do I have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee? You can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: / - How do I know, how much I have to pay? You can see how much you have to pay in the current semester in u:space at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship. There are also differences between degree programmes and non-degree programmes. - [Detailed info about the amount of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) - Where can I check, if I have successfully paid or re-enroled in my degree programme? This is how you can check if your payment was successful and you have re-enroled in your studies at the University of Vienna: 1. You can find your student documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space at Personal matters > My documents. Choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents". 2. The status of the fee in u:space > Financial matters is "received"- 3. Choose your degree programme in u:space > Studies > Study overview. There you can see the following text with a green check mark: "Re-enrolled. You are re-enrolled for your degree programme for summer/winter semester 20xx (valid until xx.xx.xxxx)". - I am completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text:
Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester. Thereby you automatically re-enrol in your studies. If you have already paid at another Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your degree programme yourself.

- You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period.
- [How much you have to pay](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and type of studies.
- Pay the exact fee (neither too much nor too little). You can find the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
- Wait until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Then you can download your student documents in u:space and validate your student ID, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).
- If you pay at another Austrian university of university of applied sciences, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself via u:space each semester.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

reranked.title: Tuition fee/Students' Union fee
reranked.text:
As long as you study, you must pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. In the first semester, you complete the admission procedure by paying the fee. In later semesters you thereby re-register for your degree programme.

Choose the applicable question below. You will be forwarded to a new page where you can find the detailed information you need.

[## How much do I have to pay?](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/)

[## How can I pay?](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/)

[## How do I apply for remission or reimbursement?](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/)

[## Am I eligible for equal status with EU/EEA citizens?](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/)

If you are currently attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU), you

- first pay the course fee at the VWU and afterwards
- the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

[Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- [Decree regarding the tuition fee 2004](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010724)
- [§ 23 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/studienrecht/)
- [Students' Union Act 2014](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)
- [Decree on Groups of Persons 2018](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010594)

- [Information on the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/)
- [Application and admission periods](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Administrative fee for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/administration-fee/)

reranked.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
reranked.text:
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee each semester within the payment period. The amount of the tuition fee does not depend on your citizenship. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Please note: a non-degree programme does not lead to a graduation with an academic degree.

You are in Austria with a residence permit? Please contact the MA 35 if examinations from a non-degree programme can be recognised for your residence permit. Supplementary examinations from the VWU are recognised.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How much is the tuition fee?
reranked.text:
Students who reside in Austria as asylum seekers, recognised refugees (persons entitled to asylum, not displaced persons), or people granted subsidiary protection may take a discounted German language course in the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) if they fulfil all admission requirements. You will be admitted as a non-degree student while you attend the VWU. During this time, you are only required to pay the [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).

Otherwise, the tuition fee for degree programmes depends on your citizenship. Asylum seekers, convention refugees, and people granted subsidiary protection may file for [equal status with EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) and, if granted, are then exempt from paying the tuition fee for the standard duration of your study programme + two tolerance semesters for a bachelor/diploma or master programme. During this time, you are only required to pay the Students’ Union fee.

Before commencing your studies, please consider how starting a degree programme may impact your financial situation or benefits. Different regulations apply depending on your student or resident status.

- [Allowance ("Grundversorgung") for asylum seekers](https://www.fluechtlinge.wien/grundversorgung/) (in German)
- [Needs-based minimum benefit for recognised refugees and people granted subsidiary protection](https://www.wien.gv.at/gesundheit/leistungen/mindestsicherung/) (in German)
- [Study grant for recognised refugees by the Austrian Study Grant Authority](https://www.stipendium.at/stipendien/studienbeihilfe) (in German)
- [Austrian Database for Scholarships and Research Grants](https://grants.at/en/)

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities
reranked.text:
As long as you attend the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) you have to pay the course fee at the VWU first. Only then you can pay the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

- [Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

reranked.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
reranked.text: can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree programme at the University of Vienna. Please note: If a higher fee is prescribed at one university, it is best to pay there directly. If you pay at the university where only the Students' Union fee is prescribed, you have to separately pay the remaining difference at the other university. For example: You only pay the Students' Union fee at another university. At the University of Vienna you have already exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. A few days after paying at the other university you can verify in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee if the Students' Union fee has already arrived. Then you pay the prescribed tuition fee at the University of Vienna. You only have to pay the tuition fee, the Students' Union fee is forwarded by the other university. - I am studying a degree programme and a continuing education or training programme at the Postgraduate Center. How much do I have to pay? If you only have to pay the Students' Union fee, you pay it with the programme fee for the Postgraduate Center. You do not have to

reranked.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
reranked.text: / - I am EU/EEA citizen. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? Check [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Study overview to see which degree programmes you are admitted to. As soon as you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna all degree programmes are continued. It does not matter if you actively study them or not. The tuition fee is prescribed if you exceed the stipulated degree programme duration plus tolerance semesters. If a degree programme that you (currently) do not want to continue is in your study overview, [you can close it](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/). The amount of the tuition fee will then be calculated anew. - I have equal status. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? If you have equal status with EU/EEA citizens you are exempt from the tuition fee as long as - your residence permit is valid and - you are within the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. [Detailed information about equal status with EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) - Will my Erasmus+ semester be counted for the tuition-free period for EU/EEA citizens? Semesters during which you are abroad with a mobility programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union fee has been paid to the University of Vienna. If you pay at an FH: - Pay the prescribed fee. - Wait for a week. - Check if the Students' Union fee has been marked as "received" in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. - If this is the case you can [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at Studies > Study overview. Do not forget: If the tuition fee is also prescribed for you at the University of Vienna you also have to pay the remaining difference. - Should I pay with some kind of express payment, so the money is transferred faster? No, this is not necessary. The transfer is not much faster that way. An express payment does not really affect the process. The fastest way is paying directly via eps-payment (only possible with Austrian bank account) or credit card in u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students'

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. - Only ever use your latest unique payment reference when paying. Otherwise, we are not able to correctly allocate your payment. Your unique payment reference has 12 digits and consists of numbers and letters. You can find all data you need for the payment in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Your name, student ID number and semester are not relevant. If you pay via eps-payment or credit card via u:space all required data is already filled in. Do not change it. - Do not save your unique payment reference. The data changes each semester. - You can only pay within the payment period of the respective semester. Thereby you re-enrol in your studies. We recommend: Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the beginning of the payment period. Thereby you have the opportunity to act in good time, in case of an incorrect payment. - If the prescribed fee is not received at the University of Vienna in due time, your studies will be closed.

reranked.title: Remission and reimbursement
reranked.text:
If you study at the University of Vienna you have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester in due time. If certain grounds are at hand, you can submit an application for remission. If you have already paid, you can apply for a reimbursement of the tuition fee provided you fulfil the applicable reasons.

If one of the reasons mentioned below applies to you, you submit your application for remission of the tuition fee within the application period.

We recommend: Submit your application for remission no later than one month before the deadline. The processing of your application may take several weeks. We will inform you about the outcome of your application as well as any further steps that may be required via e-mail to your u:account e-mail address.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

reranked.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
reranked.text: the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - Application for leave of absence granted After paying the tuition fee your [application for leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) was granted? A reimbursement is only possible if you have submitted your application for leave of absence before 30 November (for the winter semester) and 30 April (for the summer semester). - Application for remission granted Your application for remission of the tuition fee was granted after paying the tuition fee? The tuition fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too high You have paid a too high fee? The difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too little You have paid too little and are therefore no longer admitted at the University of Vienna? The fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - Reimbursement of the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is

reranked.text: If you study at multiple universities in Austria, you can decide where you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee yourself. You have already paid at another university? Then you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself each semester. Otherwise your studies will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. / - How do I re-enrol in my studies if I pay at another university? 1. Pay the prescribed fee at the other university and wait for a few days. 2. Log into u:space within the payment period of the University of Vienna. 3. Verify if the fee is already marked as "received" at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. There may be a remaining difference left to pay. This may be the case if you have, for example, paid at an Austrian university of applied science but the tuition fee is prescribed at the University of Vienna. 4. You have accidently paid the Students' Union fee twice at the University of Vienna? Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges that will be deducted from your account! → Solution: Pay the fee again. Make sure to enter the correct data in the correct field. 2. You have paid a too high/too little fee. If you have paid too much (double payment, wrong prescribed fee) we can reimburse the difference to the required fee. → Solution: Submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). If you have paid to little you can pay the remaining difference until the end of the payment period. Make sure to enter the correct payment data. If the fee is not paid fully or in due time your degree programme will be closed. → Solution: Pay the remaining difference before the end of the payment period. 3. You have entered the unique payment reference (called "Semesterkundendaten" before) of a wrong semester If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges

selected.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
selected.text:
There is a tuition fee and a Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and on whether you are enrolled in a degree programme or non-degree programme. The amount of the Students' Union fee is directly determined by the Austrian Students' Union. All students in Austria pay the Students' Union fee each semester to re-enrol in their degree programme and continue their studies.

To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Citizens of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from paying the tuition fee for a specified time period. This means: You only have to pay the Students' Union fee during the stipulated degree programme duration plus two tolerance semesters

Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can check how many semesters of the tuition-free period you have left in u:space > Studies > Study overview.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

selected.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
selected.text: payment form or online banking the processing may take up to seven days. You can check when your payment is registered at the University of Vienna at u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. As soon as the fee is marked as "received" it is registered as paid. Only then you can download your student documents. Make sure to choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents. If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another Austrian educational institution, you have to [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at the University of Vienna yourself within the payment period. Only then you can download your student documents. - What happens if I pay too late or too little? If you do not pay the full fee until the end of the payment period your degree programme will be automatically closed. You have realised that you have paid too little in due time (= within the payment period)? Transfer the remaining difference as soon as possible with the correct payment data. The required data (IBAN and BIC of the University of Vienna and your unique payment reference) is available in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You are not able to or do not want to pay the remaining difference? Please contact us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/706). We can remit the part of the fee you have already

selected.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
selected.text: If you study at the University of Vienna (and not any other Austrian educational institution) you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee here each semester. You do not have to complete any other steps. As soon as the prescribed fee has been received, you are re-enrolled in your studies. Then you can download your student documents in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) and validate your [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) (or order and then validate it if it is your first semester). / - Which payment options are available at the University of Vienna? You have four options how you can pay your tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna: 1. Eps-payment via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via eps-payment. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. The eps-payment is only possible with Austrian bank account. 2. Credit card (only Visa and Mastercard) via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via credit card. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. In the info box in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee you can find all data you need

assemble.context:
[1] Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
There is a tuition fee and a Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and on whether you are enrolled in a degree programme or non-degree programme. The amount of the Students' Union fee is directly determined by the Austrian Students' Union. All students in Austria pay the Students' Union fee each semester to re-enrol in their degree programme and continue their studies.

To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Citizens of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from paying the tuition fee for a specified time period. This means: You only have to pay the Students' Union fee during the stipulated degree programme duration plus two tolerance semesters

Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can check how many semesters of the tuition-free period you have left in u:space > Studies > Study overview.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

[2] Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) — FAQ
payment form or online banking the processing may take up to seven days. You can check when your payment is registered at the University of Vienna at u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. As soon as the fee is marked as "received" it is registered as paid. Only then you can download your student documents. Make sure to choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents. If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another Austrian educational institution, you have to [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at the University of Vienna yourself within the payment period. Only then you can download your student documents. - What happens if I pay too late or too little? If you do not pay the full fee until the end of the payment period your degree programme will be automatically closed. You have realised that you have paid too little in due time (= within the payment period)? Transfer the remaining difference as soon as possible with the correct payment data. The required data (IBAN and BIC of the University of Vienna and your unique payment reference) is available in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You are not able to or do not want to pay the remaining difference? Please contact us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/706). We can remit the part of the fee you have already

[3] Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) — I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
If you study at the University of Vienna (and not any other Austrian educational institution) you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee here each semester. You do not have to complete any other steps. As soon as the prescribed fee has been received, you are re-enrolled in your studies. Then you can download your student documents in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) and validate your [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) (or order and then validate it if it is your first semester). / - Which payment options are available at the University of Vienna? You have four options how you can pay your tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna: 1. Eps-payment via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via eps-payment. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. The eps-payment is only possible with Austrian bank account. 2. Credit card (only Visa and Mastercard) via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via credit card. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. In the info box in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee you can find all data you need

[4] Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) — I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. In the info box in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee you can find all data you need to do so. This is the IBAN and BIC of the University of Vienna and your unique payment reference. It may take up to seven days until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Please note: Your unique payment reference changes each semester. Only ever use the latest data. 4. Payment form Pay the fee with a payment form. You will receive a form in your bank. In u:space > Personal matters > My documents > Payment request/confirmation you can find all data you need for the payment. Follow the instructions in the template to fill out the payment form. It may take up to seven days until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Please note: Your unique payment reference changes each semester. Only ever use the latest data. - What do I have to keep in mind when paying at the University of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be

[5] Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee — FAQ
programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the University of Vienna. You therefore are also able to take examinations from courses at the University of Vienna that took place during your semester abroad (e.g. at a later examination date). If you have to pay the tuition fee, you can apply for [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) during your semester abroad. This is only possible during the semester in which your stay abroad takes place, not in retrospect. - I have dual citizenship. Which tuition fee do I have to pay? You can only enter one citizenship in u:space. You have one citizenship from an EU/EAA country or Switzerland and another from a non-EU/EEA country? Enter the citizenship that is more beneficial for you. If your citizenship has changed, please message us for a [change of personal data](/en/change-of-personal-data/). - I study at two public universities in Austria. Where do I have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee? You can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree

[6] Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) — FAQ
/ - How do I know, how much I have to pay? You can see how much you have to pay in the current semester in u:space at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship. There are also differences between degree programmes and non-degree programmes. - [Detailed info about the amount of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) - Where can I check, if I have successfully paid or re-enroled in my degree programme? This is how you can check if your payment was successful and you have re-enroled in your studies at the University of Vienna: 1. You can find your student documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space at Personal matters > My documents. Choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents". 2. The status of the fee in u:space > Financial matters is "received"- 3. Choose your degree programme in u:space > Studies > Study overview. There you can see the following text with a green check mark: "Re-enrolled. You are re-enrolled for your degree programme for summer/winter semester 20xx (valid until xx.xx.xxxx)". - I am completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
There is a tuition fee and a Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and on whether you are enrolled in a degree programme or non-degree programme. The amount of the Students' Union fee is directly determined by the Austrian Students' Union. All students in Austria pay the Students' Union fee each semester to re-enrol in their degree programme and continue their studies.

To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Citizens of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from paying the tuition fee for a specified time period. This means: You only have to pay the Students' Union fee during the stipulated degree programme duration plus two tolerance semesters

Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can check how many semesters of the tuition-free period you have left in u:space > Studies > Study overview.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

[2] Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) — FAQ
payment form or online banking the processing may take up to seven days. If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another Austrian educational institution, you have to [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at the University of Vienna yourself within the payment period. Only then you can download your student documents. - What happens if I pay too late or too little? If you do not pay the full fee until the end of the payment period your degree programme will be automatically closed.
